rows.text:
ICE’s first interaction with Alejandro Paulino-Espidio was in 2006, when he was found among 17 illegal aliens hiding in the back of a van. He was granted voluntary return to Mexico, but that wasn’t the last ICE would see of him.

He snuck back into the United States by climbing a border fence in 2018. When Border Patrol agents found him, he claimed he planned to live with some friends in Orange County, California. Federal officials deported him that time.

But he returned illegally — again. 

Then, in 2022, a California judge in Fresno County convicted him of arson. A few years later, a California judge in Merced County convicted him of escaping from custody and vandalism.

➡️ICE deported Paulino-Espidio May 16. His repeated disregard of Americans boundaries, laws and safety shows clearly why @DHSgov has prioritized immigration enforcement and removal AND border security.

ICE’s first interaction with Alejandro Paulino-Espidio was in 2006, when he was found among 17 illegal aliens hiding in the back of a van. He was granted voluntary return to Mexico, but that wasn’t the last ICE would see of him. He snuck back into the United States by climbing a border fence in 2018. When Border Patrol agents found him, he claimed he planned to live with some friends in Orange County, California. Federal officials deported him that time. But he returned illegally — again. Then, in 2022, a California judge in Fresno County convicted him of arson. A few years later, a California judge in Merced County convicted him of escaping from custody and vandalism. ➡️ICE deported Paulino-Espidio May 16. His repeated disregard of Americans boundaries, laws and safety shows clearly why @DHSgov has prioritized immigration enforcement and removal AND border security.

Irving, Texas, police charged Magdaleno Roman-Rosales, of Mexico, with kidnapping many years ago and he ultimately received a 120-day sentence for unlawful restraint. He was subsequently removed to his home country. At some point, he returned to the United States illegally. 🔎ICE @ERODallas Fugitive Operations arrested him during a targeted immigration enforcement operation near his residence May 8. During the arrest, he put our officers, the public and himself in danger by attempting to flee. 🚨He backed his truck into an ICE vehicle. Before he was able to drive away, skilled ICE officers were able to gain entry into the vehicle. Then, Roman-Rosales refused to exit his truck and had to be physically removed from the cab. He continued to resist his arrest at every step. Thankfully, no one was seriously injured during the operation. ➡️He will stay in ICE custody pending prosecution.
